sglang.title: fix: handle None multimodal_inputs during merging and filtering batches in disaggregation decode mode
sglang.author: GaoYusong
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

In disaggregation decode mode, ScheduleBatch.prepare_for_extend is not called, leaving multimodal_inputs uninitialized for non-multimodal models. This fix guards against None to prevent errors during batch merging and filtering.

sglang.title: Handle empty input string for embedding models
sglang.author: ravi03071991
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
This PR introduces input validation to the /v1/embeddings endpoint to prevent errors when processing empty or whitespace-only inputs.
Sending semantically empty inputs (such as "", " ", [], [""], [" "], [[]]) to the embedding endpoint can cause unexpected behavior or potentially lead to d

sglang.title: [CI] Reorganize the 8 gpu tests
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Remove unit-test-backend-pd and merge it into unittest-test-backend-8-gpu
Do not need to pass all unit-test-backend-1-gpu to run unittest-test-backend-8-gpu. This is because unit-test-backend-1-gpu is flaky and we want to run unittest-test-backend-8-gpu earlier.

sglang.title: Replace time.time() to time.perf_counter() for benchmarking.
sglang.author: lifuhuang
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Currently, SGL measures duration using wall clock (time.time()), which is not recommended for interval measuring purpose as it does not guarantee monotonicity (e.g., due to NTP sync) and has lower resolution. perf_counter should be used instead for perf measurement puropses. More details

sglang.title: Performing Vocabulary Parallelism for LM Head across Attention TP Groups
sglang.author: ch-wan
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

This PR allows users to set --enable-dp-lm-head for accelerating lm_head for DP attention. The original implementation adopts vocabulary parallel across the entire TP group, which has two issues under DP attention:

The memory requirement is proportional to the DP size, which may prevent

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Add revision to `transformers.Auto*.from_pretrained` processors

Fix a rare care where specifying --revision does not change the behavior of huggingface processors (i.e. multi-modal video/image processors)
Seems like revision is being honored in all places other than vllm/transformers_utils/processor.py
~/projects/vllm$ grep -rin -l "\.from_pretrained(" ./vllm/
.

vllm.title: [Perf] Use small max_num_batched_tokens for A100
vllm.author: KuntaiDu
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR solves a performance regression issue on A100. Related PR: #17073




Token throughput (Before this PR)
Token throughput (After this PR)




Llama 70B, TP=2
1054.01
1438.49   (+36%)


Llama 70B, TP=4
3053.03
2994.03 (-2%)



vLLM launching command:
TP=2:
vllm serve meta-llama/Llama-3.1-70B-I

vllm.title: [Bugfix]: v1 engine - consider lora adapters in allowed_token_ids
vllm.author: bbrowning
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Previously, the v1 engine was only look at the base model's tokenizer to determine the range of token id values that were valid in allowed_token_ids. Now, it takes into account any additional tokens added by any lora adapters in use for this request.
The existing v0 behavior already took this into a

vllm.title: [Bugfix] validate grammar and throw 400 error instead of crashing the engine when xgrammar validation fails
vllm.author: Jason-CKY
vllm.bodyExcerpt: API server crashes when xgrammar receives an invalid grammar when the guided_decoding_backend is set to auto. This can be triggered via an invalid json schema, regex, or EBNF grammar. This is due to the assumption that any validation error raised by xgrammar is due to the unsupported json schema fea
